checks.finding: design.md justifies the routing order through the checked-in attempt matrix and tie-break sequence in Model Selection, explains immutable snapshots and closed manifests through the integrity boundaries in Manifest Contract and Security Boundaries, and explains why no feature flag or migration order applies in Deployment / CI Plan.
design.md contains no unresolved open questions; implementation-blocking decisions are specified directly in Model Selection, Manifest Contract, Deployment / CI Plan, and Compatibility and Rollback.

The two assumptions in design.md are bounded technical defaults: Bash, jq, and PowerShell are the declared repository/CI stack, while unavailable runtimes fail closed; host capability metadata is required by REQ-002 and absence likewise fails closed rather than being silently assumed.

design.md Constraint Compliance explicitly covers turn-first ordering, both provider families, role floors, script-only deterministic work, human-only terminal resume, context isolation, bounded fallback, runtime parity, and synchronized 1.5.0 release metadata, matching REQ-001 through REQ-011 without contradiction.
No performance requirements found in requirements.md.

design.md Deployment / CI Plan identifies the repository/plugin release target, requires seven GitHub Actions jobs, states that no variables, secrets, service deployment, or database migration are introduced, and explains why feature flags and migration order are not applicable to the atomic release.

design.md Data Plan states that no database or application-data migration is required, preserves historical reports without migration, and defines atomic publication plus a pinned rollback inventory restoring the 1.4.0 baseline.

design.md declares no network API, RPC, event, service, secret, or network dependency. The repository-local host-adapter boundary is identified in Backend Plan, its capability-registry contract is defined in Model Selection and API / Contract Plan, and unavailable capability data fails closed as model-tier-unavailable.

design.md Components and Constraint Compliance cover every requested routing, model-equivalence, isolation, handoff, reporting, parity, release, and rollback capability from requirements.md; the design explicitly excludes services, databases, frontend, and network APIs and introduces no unrelated product surface.

design.md Test Strategy names concrete tests for routing, risk validation, terminal resume, manifest tampering, reviewer/evaluator isolation, Bash/PowerShell parity, metrics, rollback, and full repository regression; Security Boundaries and Compatibility and Rollback tie those checks to the high-risk integrity and rollback claims.
